sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see something very interesting! When I moved the right agent RIGHT (which has c center), several things happened:
1. The left agent (which was inside the left c-frame) got KICKED OUT and moved to cols 15-17 (right next to the c-frame)
2. The white square that was inside the c-frame reappeared at cols 12-14
3. The left agent now has W center again
4. The left c-frame's border changed from c (16 pixels) to c (13 pixels) - it's now incomplete!
5. The right agent stayed at cols 30-32 with c center
So moving the right agent RIGHT caused the left agent to be pushed out of the left c-frame! This is a chain reaction mechanic.
Wait, but the left c-frame border changed from 16 pixels to 13 pixels. Let me look at the ASCII:
```
Row 32: gwwcccccwwwwww  -> c-frame top border still intact
Row 33: gwwcwwwNNNwwww  -> left side c, then www, then NNN (agent)
Row 34: gwwcwwwNWNwwww  -> left side c, then www, then NWN (agent)
Row 35: gwwcwwwNNNwwww  -> left side c, then www, then NNN (agent)
Row 36: gwwcccccwwwwww  -> c-frame bottom border still intact
```
So the c-frame lost its right side! The frame was at cols 11-15, and now the right wall (col 15) is missing. The agent is at cols 15-17, which overlaps where the right wall was.
So the mechanic seems to be:
- When an agent with c center moves RIGHT, it pushes/kicks out the agent in the left c-frame
- The kicked agent loses its c center and goes back to W
- The c-frame's wall is broken
This is getting complex. Let me think about what the goal might be.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider. The right agent didn't move - it's still at cols 30-32. But moving it RIGHT caused changes elsewhere. So the RIGHT action doesn't just move the active agent - it has some global effect.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe:
- UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT don't move the active agent
- They trigger some kind of global transformation based on which direction and which agent is active
Let me test this hypothesis by trying different directions.
Actually, looking back at my earlier moves:
- I moved the left agent LEFT several times and it did move left
- Then I moved it UP and it moved up
- Then I moved it RIGHT and it moved right into the c-frame
- Then I moved the right agent LEFT and it moved left
- Then I moved the right agent RIGHT and it moved right
So the agents DO move when I press direction keys. But in the last move, moving the right agent RIGHT also caused the left agent to be kicked out of the c-frame.
Wait, let me re-examine. The right agent was at cols 30-32. I pressed RIGHT. The agent should move to cols 31-33. But it's still at cols 30-32. So the agent didn't actually move! Instead, something else happened.
Hmm, but the animation changed bbox was [33, 12, 63, 50], which includes the left c-frame area. So the action affected the left c-frame, not the right agent.
Maybe the RIGHT action with the right agent active doesn't move the agent (because it's blocked by the purple bar), but instead triggers some other effect.
Or maybe the agent DID try to move right but hit the purple bar, and hitting the bar triggered the chain reaction.
Let me try moving the right agent RIGHT again to see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The right agent didn't move but the left agent was kicked out of the c-frame
# Let me try moving RIGHT again to see the pattern
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
